amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The E. coli Stringent Response is a crucial global regulatory mechanism that allows bacteria to adapt to various nutrient limitations, ensuring survival during periods of stress. Triggered primarily by amino acid starvation, but also by fatty acid, nitrogen, or glucose phosphate limitations, the response orchestrates a metabolic shift from anabolism to catabolism. The core of this response is the rapid synthesis of the alarmone (p)ppGpp (guanosine tetraphosphate and pentaphosphate) by the enzymes RelA and SpoT. RelA is activated when uncharged tRNAs bind to the A-site of ribosomes stalled during translation, leading to its (p)ppGpp synthetase activity. SpoT, a bifunctional enzyme, can also synthesize (p)ppGpp under specific conditions (e.g., fatty acid limitation) and hydrolyze it when nutrients are abundant.
mermaid:
graph TD
    subgraph Triggers & Initial Sensing
        A[Amino acid starvation]:::red
        B[Fatty acid limitation]:::red
        C[Nitrogen limitation]:::red
        D[Glucose phosphate stress]:::red
        E[Other nutrient limitation]:::red

        A --> FZ
        B --> FZ
        C --> FZ
        D --> FZ
        E --> FZ
        FZ{Nutrient limitation present?}:::orange

        FZ -->|Yes| F[Uncharged tRNA accumulation]:::blue
        F --> G[Ribosome stalling]:::green
    end

    subgraph (p)ppGpp Synthesis
        H[RelA protein]:::yellow
        J[GTP substrate]:::blue
        K[ATP substrate]:::blue

        G & H & J & K --> GA[RelA activation requirements met?]:::lavender
        GA --> I[RelA binds A-site of stalled ribosome]:::green
        I --> M[RelA (p)ppGpp synthetase activity]:::green
        M --> O[pppGpp formation]:::blue
        O --> N[pppGpp converted to ppGpp]:::green

        P[SpoT protein]:::yellow
        PA[Fatty acid starvation or other signals?]:::orange
        PA -->|Yes| Q[SpoT (p)ppGpp synthetase activity]:::green
        Q --> N

        N --> S[(p)ppGpp levels rise]:::blue
        M --> L[PPi release]:::blue
        Q --> L
    end

    subgraph (p)ppGpp Action & Downstream Effects
        S --> T[RNA Polymerase (RNAP)]:::yellow
        V[DksA protein]:::yellow
        S & T & V --> WA[(p)ppGpp-RNAP-DksA complex formed?]:::lavender
        WA --> W[DksA-ppGpp-RNAP complex]:::green

        W --> YZ[/Which anabolic process to repress?/]:::orange
        YZ -->|rRNA synthesis| X[rRNA gene promoters]:::blue
        YZ -->|tRNA synthesis| AA[tRNA gene promoters]:::blue
        YZ -->|DNA replication| AC[oriC DNA]:::blue
        YZ -->|Fatty acid synthesis| AH[Fatty acid synthesis genes]:::blue
        YZ -->|Phospholipid synthesis| AI[Phospholipid synthesis genes]:::blue

        X --> Y[Decreased rRNA transcription]:::green
        Y --> Z[Reduced ribosome biogenesis]:::violet

        AA --> AB[Decreased tRNA transcription]:::green

        AC --> AD[Modulation of DNA gyrase activity]:::green
        AD --> AE[Increased negative supercoiling at oriC]:::blue
        AE --> AF[/Inhibition of DnaA binding to oriC/]:::green
        AF --> AG[/Inhibition of DNA replication initiation/]:::violet

        AH & AI --> AJ[/Repression of anabolic pathways/]:::green
        AJ --> AK[Reduced cell growth]:::violet

        W --> YB[Which catabolic/stress response to activate?]:::orange
        YB -->|Amino acid biosynthesis| AL[Amino acid biosynthesis genes]:::blue
        YB -->|Nitrogen assimilation| AO[Nitrogen assimilation genes]:::blue
        YB -->|General stress response| AR[General stress response genes]:::blue
        YB -->|Catabolic pathways| AU[Catabolic gene promoters]:::blue

        AL --> AM[Activation of amino acid biosynthesis]:::green
        AM --> AN[Increased amino acid pools]:::violet

        AO --> AP[Activation of nitrogen assimilation]:::green
        AP --> AQ[Enhanced nitrogen uptake/metabolism]:::violet

        AR --> AS[Activation of stress regulons]:::green
        AS --> AT[Enhanced stress resistance]:::violet

        AU --> AV[Activation of catabolic pathways]:::green
        AV --> AW[Utilization of alternative carbon sources]:::violet
    end

    subgraph Recovery
        AX[Nutrient availability restored]:::red
        AX --> AY[Charged tRNA levels increase]:::blue
        AY --> AZ[RelA activity decreases]:::green
        AZ --> BA[SpoT (p)ppGpp hydrolase activity dominant]:::green
        BA --> BB[(p)ppGpp levels decrease]:::blue
        BB --> BC[Transition to growth phase]:::violet
        BB --> BD[Recovery from glucose phosphate stress]:::violet
    end

sources.title: The Stringent Response Inhibits DNA Replication Initiation in E. coli by Modulating Supercoiling of oriC.
Ribosome•RelA structures reveal the mechanism of stringent response activation.
Nitrogen stress response and stringent response are coupled in Escherichia coli.
Stringent Response Regulators Contribute to Recovery from Glucose Phosphate Stress in Escherichia coli.
